name: Galaktoboureko
recipeIngredient: ¾ cup Butter, melted and hot
recipeInstructions:
1. In a heavy-bottomed, 3-quart saucepan, gradually bring the milk to a boil; ensure it does not scorch.
2. Sift together the semolina, cornstarch, 1 cup of sugar, and salt. Gradually add this mixture to the boiling milk, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon.
3. Cook over medium heat until the mixture thickens and comes to a full boil. Remove from heat.
4. Beat eggs on high speed in an electric mixer. Gradually add half a cup of sugar, continuing to beat until very thick and fluffy (about 10 minutes).
5. Add vanilla extract to the beaten eggs.
6. Stirring constantly, slowly add the egg mixture to the hot pudding.
7. Partially cover the pan and allow it to cool.
8. Butter a 9 x 12 x 3-inch baking pan, covering the bottom with 7 sheets of filo, brushing generously with butter between each sheet. Ensure some filo extends up the sides.
9. Pour the custard over the filo in the pan. Cover with the remaining 5 sheets, brushing butter between each and on the surface.
10. With a very sharp knife's tip, score the top filo into square or diamond shapes, being careful not to cut as deeply as the custard.
11. Bake in a moderate (350°F) oven on the center rack for 40-45 minutes, until crisp and golden chestnut in color and the custard is firm.
12. Meanwhile, boil the remaining sugar with water and lemon or orange peel for 5 minutes. Add brandy or Cognac if desired; set aside.
13. Remove galaktoboureko from oven and place on a cake rack. Spoon hot syrup over the entire dish, especially along the edges.
14. Allow to cool thoroughly before cutting and serving. Store in refrigerator.
